subject: Re: Re-process a single item's CTF
body:
Hey Alister,

Since we are mass pre-processing dozens of tomograms at once, it is not
feasible to keep track of the filtered out tilts and check/uncheck them
manually. But it seems the processing status is indeed preserved in xml
files from the initial pre-processing!

Thank you so much for your help! :)

Have a great day!

Cheers,

Cyan

PhD student at the Physical Chemistry Curie Lab

On Fri, 10 Dec 2021 at 11:46, Alister Burt <alisterburt@gmail.com> wrote:

> Hey Cyan,
>
> You have the option of including or not including filtered out images at
> various points, just make sure to keep an eye on those checkboxes 🙂
>
> Cheers,
>
> Alister
>
> On 10 Dec 2021, at 10:26, Cyan Ching <xingyichengcyanching@gmail.com>
> wrote:
>
> ﻿
> Hey Alister!
>
> Thank you so much for your prompt reply!
>
> What about tilts that were filtered out? Would they still be excluded for
> further steps?
>
> Cheers,
>
> Cyan
>
> PhD student at the Physical Chemistry Curie Lab
>
>
>
> On Fri, 10 Dec 2021 at 10:51, Alister Burt <alisterburt@gmail.com> wrote:
>
>> Hi Cyan,
>>
>> If you did this once the processing was finished then don’t worry, you
>> still have all of your results and appropriate metadata for the ‘outdated’
>> results in the xml files. The fact that entries are outdated shouldn’t stop
>> you from moving forward with your processing 🙂
>>
>> I don’t think there’s a way to change settings for a subset of images
>> from the GUI.
>>
>> Cheers,
>>
>> Alister
>>
>> On 10 Dec 2021, at 09:39, Cyan Ching <xingyichengcyanching@gmail.com>
>> wrote:
>>
>> ﻿
>> Dear all,
>>
>> Good morning!
>>
>> During mass frame series pre-processing in Warp, is there a way to keep
>> processing status of tilts following re-processing? All processed tilts
>> just became outdated following the re-processing of a single item's CTF.
>>
>> Thank you for your help!
>>
>> Best,
>>
>> Cyan
>>
>> PhD student at Physical Chemistry Curie Lab
>>
>> --
